title: Northrop Grumman partners with Apex on space-based interceptors for Golden Dome
summary: Northrop Grumman has partnered with Apex, a Los Angeles-based startup, to develop space-based interceptors for the Golden Dome program. Apex specializes in producing standardized, cost-effective satellite buses.

title: Spaceport facility bonds are now law – and they fundamentally change space infrastructure finance
summary: Spaceport facility bonds have been enacted into law, significantly altering the financing landscape for space infrastructure. This marks a major shift in how spaceport projects can be funded.

title: China conducts surprise launch of Long March 12B, delivers Qianfan satellites on debut flight
summary: China successfully launched its reusable Long March 12B rocket for the first time without prior notice, deploying the Qianfan satellite constellation into orbit.

title: New Glenn failure worsens constrained launch market
summary: The explosion of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket has disrupted the already constrained launch market, with the rocket potentially out of service for a year or more. This incident highlights ongoing challenges in the space industry's launch capabilities.

title: China launches test direct-to-device satellites for multiple projects
summary: China launched four new satellite internet test satellites into orbit using a hypergolic rocket. This marks the end of a busy month of launches for the country.

title: FAA documents outline SpaceX plans for Starfall reentry vehicles
summary: FAA documents reveal SpaceX's plans to develop and test Starfall reentry vehicles for potential in-space manufacturing support. The project aims to advance reusable spacecraft technology for future missions.

title: Blue Origin gets national security launch task order hours before New Glenn explosion
summary: Blue Origin received a national security launch task order from the U.S. Space Force and National Reconnaissance Office shortly before the explosion of its New Glenn rocket. The agencies reaffirmed their commitment to Blue Origin as a partner.

title: Space Force awards SpaceX $4.16 billion to build satellite network for airborne target tracking
summary: The U.S. Space Force has awarded SpaceX a $4.16 billion contract to develop a low-orbit satellite constellation for tracking airborne threats like aircraft and cruise missiles. This initiative aims to enhance national security by improving surveillance capabilities.

title: Space Force’s commercial gatekeeper offers a playbook for startups seeking defense business
summary: The U.S. Space Force's commercial gatekeeper emphasizes transparency, patience, and a clear military value proposition for startups aiming to work with the defense sector. Technical innovation alone is not sufficient for securing defense contracts.

title: Bellatrix and TelePIX plan 2028 air-breathing VLEO imaging demonstration
summary: TelePIX and Bellatrix Aerospace will collaborate on a 2028 demonstration mission to test air-breathing propulsion for very low Earth orbit (VLEO) geospatial imaging. The mission aims to validate advanced imaging capabilities in VLEO using innovative propulsion technology.

title: It had the power! The weird origins of He-Man, Skeletor, and the 'Masters of the Universe'
summary: The origins of He-Man, Skeletor, and the 'Masters of the Universe' were influenced by Mattel's response to 'Star Wars'. The franchise became a cultural phenomenon with a life of its own.

title: The 2025 hurricane season was devastating. Will 2026 be calmer?
summary: Forecasters at NOAA predict a below-normal number of named storms for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season following the devastating 2025 season. This outlook offers potential relief after a year of severe weather impacts.

title: Manhattanhenge is back! | Space photo of the day for June 1, 2026
summary: Manhattanhenge, a celestial event where the sun aligns with Manhattan's street grid, will occur in 2026 with two opportunities in June and July. The phenomenon is highlighted in a space photo of the day for June 1, 2026.

title: 3 bright planets light up June's night sky — Here's where and when to look
summary: June's night sky will feature three bright planets: Venus, Jupiter, and Mercury. This provides a spectacular opportunity for stargazers to observe these celestial bodies.

title: May's Blue Moon wows stargazers worldwide — see these stunning photos of the smallest full moon of 2026
summary: The smallest full moon of 2026, known as May's Blue Moon, captivated stargazers globally with its stunning appearance on May 31. The celestial event provided a rare visual spectacle for observers worldwide.

title: 'A pretty significant setback': How Blue Origin's rocket explosion affects NASA's moon plans
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket explosion is a major setback for the company and will impact NASA's Artemis program, which aims to return astronauts to the Moon. The incident delays critical missions and raises concerns about the rocket's reliability for lunar missions.

title: 'The Arrival' at 30: Charlie Sheen’s criminally underrated alien invasion thriller feels much scarier today
summary: The article discusses the 30th anniversary of the movie 'The Arrival', calling it a criminally underrated alien invasion thriller. It highlights how the film's themes feel more relevant today.

title: Watch this bio-inspired Mars rover concept 'swim' through sand on curved wheels (video)
summary: A bio-inspired Mars rover concept uses curved wheels inspired by the sandfish lizard to navigate sand. The design aims to improve mobility on sandy surfaces like those found on Mars.

title: Pretty in Pink
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory and James Webb Space Telescope captured an image of the Westerlund 2 star cluster, showcasing stars aged between one and three million years. The composite image combines X-ray data (pink) with infrared data to highlight the cluster's features.

title: Space Out This Summer with Variety of NASA STEM Activities
summary: NASA is offering a variety of STEM activities for summer, encouraging creativity, outdoor exploration, and future planning. The initiatives aim to engage the public in space-related learning and discovery.

title: Gravity Waves From Super Typhoon Sinlaku
summary: Satellites observed gravity waves generated by Super Typhoon Sinlaku striking upper-atmosphere phenomena. The intensifying tropical cyclone created detectable atmospheric disturbances.

title: NASA Awards Contract for Johnson Space Center Infrastructure
summary: NASA has awarded a contract to seven companies for infrastructure improvements at Johnson Space Center in Houston, with a potential value of up to $300 million. The upgrades will focus on mission-support facilities, utilities, and equipment, with funds to be obligated by September 30, 2026.

title: NASA Hosts SpaceX Crew-11 Astronauts for Public Event at Headquarters
summary: NASA will host a public event featuring three astronauts from the SpaceX Crew-11 mission at its headquarters in Washington, D.C. The event is part of the crew's postflight visit and will be held in the Webb Auditorium.

title: NASA’s Roman Space Telescope Primary Mirror Gets Last Look
summary: NASA completed the final inspection of the primary mirror for the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope. The 7.9-foot mirror will help capture detailed panoramas of space.

title: Hubble Spies Faint Irregular Galaxy
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the dwarf irregular galaxy ESO 490-017, located 23 million light-years away in the constellation Canis Major. The galaxy's low surface brightness and faint appearance are highlighted against brighter foreground stars.

title: Hubble Captures M88 on Journey to Center of Virgo Cluster
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the spiral galaxy Messier 88 (M88), located approximately 63 million light-years away. The galaxy is moving toward the center of the Virgo Cluster.

title: Painting the Growing Season in the Maize Triangle
summary: Radar data from an agricultural area in South Africa reveals crop types and seasonal changes using a vivid color palette. The data highlights variations in the Southern Hemisphere's growing season.

title: NASA’s X-59 Prepares for First Supersonic Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic research aircraft is preparing for its first supersonic flight as part of new test missions. The aircraft will undergo critical objectives, including breaking the sound barrier.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. This discovery enhances understanding of early cosmic structures.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a catastrophic failure during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36. The explosion marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, utilizing rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, including four SpaceX Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo satellite launches.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance activities throughout April. The ongoing operations highlight the station's role in supporting research and logistics in space.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to the Tiangong space station, which has been permanently occupied since June 5, 2022. This mission marks another step in China's ongoing space station operations.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, from SpaceX's facilities. The mission demonstrated significant improvements and impressed during its first flight.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, Rocket Lab's Electron launch, and Arianespace's Vega C flight.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Arianespace's Vega C rocket to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's significant milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and selecting materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for ESA's missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh perspectives.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) to advance space exploration through training, operations, simulations, and public engagement. This technology is opening new pathways for interactive and immersive space-related activities.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The article discusses a sturdy and sensitive component in the space industry. The image likely depicts this component.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test, likely related to a landing system for a spacecraft or rover. The term 'bake-out' suggests a thermal conditioning process to ensure parachute functionality.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed to securely grasp and manipulate objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal operations.

title: Bellatrix Aerospace Tapped to Build Korean VLEO Demo Sat
summary: Bellatrix Aerospace has partnered with Korean optical payload manufacturer TelePIX to build a Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) demonstration satellite, targeting a launch no earlier than 2028.

title: Opinion: Commercial Space Is Generating Signals Decision-Makers Cannot Hear
summary: Commercial space activities are producing vast amounts of operational data, but decision-makers are struggling to process these signals effectively. The current mechanism for filtering and prioritizing these signals is inadequate.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn Explodes on the Pad
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket experienced a catastrophic failure during a launch attempt, resulting in an explosion on the launch pad. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's ambitions in the space industry.

title: Europe Celebrates its Sovereignty Progress at SmallSat
summary: Europe highlighted its progress in achieving orbital sovereignty during SmallSat Europe. The event underscored the continent's advancements in independent space capabilities.

title: Observable Space Raises $90M Series A
summary: Observable Space, a space-tech startup, has raised $90 million in a Series A funding round to expand its laser and optical hardware production and international presence beyond the US.

title: Space Markets Emerges From Stealth With Coinbase Ventures Investment
summary: Space Markets has emerged from stealth mode with a new investment from Coinbase Ventures. The company aims to create futures markets for space-related commodities and resources.

title: Exclusive: Stellar Alpina Raises $4.5M to Build Rotating Detonation Engines
summary: Stellar Alpina, a European propulsion startup, has raised $4.5 million to develop rotating detonation engines, a novel technology aimed at advancing spacecraft propulsion. This funding will support the company's efforts to bring this innovative engine design to market.

title: Aitech Upgrades its Space Supercomputer
summary: Aitech has upgraded its space supercomputer by integrating NVIDIA's IGX Thor platform into the S-A2300 COTS AI Supercomputer, enhancing in-orbit data processing capabilities for customers.

title: NASA Awards LTV, Lunar Transport Contracts
summary: NASA has awarded contracts for Lunar Terrain Vehicles (LTV) and lunar transport services, marking a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon. The announcement was made during a press conference at NASA Headquarters.

title: Op-ed: Commercial Demand in LEO is Lacking. The Government Can Help.
summary: A recent op-ed argues that the most promising market opportunity in Low Earth Orbit (LEO)—in-space manufacturing—lacks sufficient commercial demand to sustain itself, and calls for government support to drive growth.

title: SpaceX Gets Nearly $4.2 Billion Nod From Space Force For Initial SB-AMTI
summary: SpaceX received a nearly $4.2 billion contract from the U.S. Space Force for the initial phase of the SB-AMTI program. This follows a $2.3 billion award for the Space Data Network Backbone, a proliferated Low Earth Orbit communications system.

title: Sateliot Wins New Telenor IoT Deal
summary: Sateliot, a Spanish satellite company, has secured a new IoT partnership with Telenor IoT, a division of the Telenor Group, a major Nordic telecommunications provider.

title: Viasat Full Year ‘26 Takeaways: Record Revenue, Equatys Moves Forward
summary: Viasat reported record revenue and awards in fiscal year 2026, though it fell short of stabilization goals in maritime and fixed broadband sectors. The company highlighted progress with its Equatys initiative despite these challenges.

title: Blue Origin’s Launch Pad Explosion Could Impact Timelines for Commercial Customers, NASA Missions
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered an explosion on its launch pad at Cape Canaveral, potentially delaying commercial and NASA missions. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's spaceflight timelines.

title: EU Targets User Terminal Tech Advancements Ahead of IRIS²
summary: The European Union has issued a tender for innovative and cost-effective user terminal technologies for the IRIS² satellite constellation. The call for proposals aims to advance user terminal tech ahead of the constellation's deployment.

title: BlackSky Advances Non-Earth Imaging Business With 7-Figure Renewal
summary: BlackSky Technology secured a seven-figure contract renewal for non-Earth imagery services, where its satellites capture images of objects in orbit. This highlights the growing demand for space situational awareness and orbital debris monitoring.

title: UK’s O2 Satellite Expands D2D Service to iPhones
summary: UK’s O2 Satellite has expanded its direct-to-device (D2D) service to support iPhones, utilizing SpaceX’s Starlink Mobile constellation. The service was initially launched earlier this year for select devices.

title: Swissto12 Adds to Leadership Team to Target Global Growth
summary: Swissto12 is expanding its leadership team with new appointments, including a chief commercial officer and executives for government affairs and partnerships, to drive global growth.

title: Orbital Data Centers Must Tackle Chip Lifespan, Launch Availability, and Cybersecurity Challenges
summary: Orbital data centers face challenges like chip lifespan, launch availability, and cybersecurity. The discussion is highlighted in the context of SpaceX's initial IPO documents.

title: Will the European Launch Market Finally Ignite?
summary: The European space industry is seeking more efficient and reliable access to space, a major topic at a recent launch panel. The discussion highlights the urgency for advancements in this area.
